ip_outcome_note: Vision pass did not flag a suspected_ip. The depicted figure is canonically Garima, a non-planeswalker secondary character on the Aether Rangers racing team (Wizards-internal Avishkar lore, no external IP risk). No frontmatter changes.

trivia_bullets.text: Aetherdrift is set during the Ghirapur Grand Prix, an interplanar race hosted on Avishkar (the plane formerly called Kaladesh, renamed after its post-Consulate revolution). The Aether Rangers are Avishkar's home team, described in Wizards' official Planeswalker's Guide as 'a cross-generation, cross-class team of comrades that combine the best of pre-revolutionary Avishkar with the hope-chasing cohort of the new generation,' co-led by Chief Engineer Pia Nalaar. [Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1]
sources: Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Garima was delighted to finally test out her design. The amazing views were a bonus." [Scryfall]. Garima is canonically identified by MTG Wiki as a member of the Aether Rangers whose sole card appearance is this one — the engineer is literally test-piloting her own design. [MTG Wiki: List of secondary characters/Avishkar; MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers (Depicted in)]
sources: MTG Wiki: List of secondary characters/Avishkar

trivia_bullets.text: Art by Elizabeth Peiro, a Barcelona-based freelance illustrator with credits across MTG and other TCG/TTRPG work; this is one of her Aetherdrift contributions. [Scryfall; Elizabeth Peiro portfolio site]

Skystreak Engineer is a {1}{U} 1/3 Human Pilot with flying and Exhaust — {4}{U}: put two +1/+1 counters on this creature. Exhaust is Aetherdrift's signature once-per-game activated-ability mechanic; per designer Mark Rosewater, the mechanic was originally codenamed 'turbo' and evokes a one-time nitro switch, kept as 'exhaust' so it could be reused in non-racing sets. [Wizards: Aetherdrift Mechanics; MTG Rocks: Exhaust mechanic interview]
sources: MTG Rocks: Exhaust mechanic is here to stay

trivia_bullets.text: Limited sentiment: Draftsim's Aetherdrift Limited Set Review rates the card 2.5/10, calling it 'a weak early game flier that can turn into a reasonable threat later on. Best if you have exhaust incentives.' Frank Karsten's Ultimate Guard analysis ranks it fourth-best among common exhaust creatures behind Stampeding Scurryfoot, Hazard of the Dunes, and Keen Buccaneer. EDHREC rank 25,024 indicates very low Commander adoption. [Draftsim: Ultimate Aetherdrift Limited Set Review; Ultimate Guard: Aetherdrift Limited top cards; Scryfall edhrec_rank]

related_cards.name: Pia Nalaar, Chief Mechanic
related_cards.note: Co-leader of the Aether Rangers and Chief Engineer; the named character anchoring the team Garima belongs to. [MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers]

related_cards.name: Sita Varma, Masked Racer
related_cards.note: The Aether Rangers' star pilot (the masked folk hero 'Spitfire'); represents the team's other named co-captain. [MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers]

related_cards.name: Elvish Refueler
related_cards.note: Another Aether Rangers crew-member card; common/uncommon supporting-cast pattern Wizards used to populate the team beneath its named legends. [MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers (Depicted in)]

related_cards.name: Nimble Thopterist
related_cards.note: Aether Rangers supporting-cast common, also depicted in the team roster on MTG Wiki. [MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers (Depicted in)]

related_cards.name: Loxodon Surveyor
related_cards.note: Aether Rangers supporting-cast card; another mechanic/crew member in the team's depicted roster. [MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers (Depicted in)]

related_cards.name: Sabotage Strategist
related_cards.note: Aether Rangers supporting-cast card depicted in the team roster. [MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers (Depicted in)]

related_cards.name: Rangers' Aetherhive
related_cards.note: Aether Rangers associated card (team-named artifact/location). [MTG Wiki: Aether Rangers]
